title: VddEngine API takes builder_provider as &str throughout coupling to stringly-typed routing
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
naming
## Location
src/vdd/mod.rs (multiple): 209, 288, 348, 402, 803, 885, 995
## Violation
Every public/internal method in VddEngine takes builder_provider: &str:

```rust
pub async fn review_text(..., builder_provider: &str, builder_api_key: ...) -> ...
pub async fn process_response(..., builder_provider: &str, ...) -> ...
async fn advisory_review(..., builder_provider: &str, ...) -> ...
async fn blocking_loop(..., builder_provider: &str, ...) -> ...
async fn triage_findings(..., builder_provider: &str, ...) -> ...
async fn verify_findings(..., builder_provider: &str, ...) -> ...
async fn send_to_builder_for_verification(..., provider_name: &str, ...) -> ...
async fn send_to_builder(..., provider_name: &str, ...) -> ...
```

This is stringly-typed routing through 8+ functions in the same module. A typo at any call site silently routes to the wrong provider (or fails downstream with a ConfigError that doesn't pinpoint the typo location). The framework's naming guideline mandates "registered constants" and "enums over stringly-typed code."

Worse, the parameter is paired with builder_api_key: Option<&ApiKey> at each callsite — these are conceptually one unit (a Provider handle) but passed as two parameters. The function-signature-mandate of 0-2 parameters is technically met but only by collapsing the unit into two coupled positional args.

## Mandated refactor
Define a Provider handle (existing ProviderAdapter trait or a new struct), pass it as a single value, and remove the &str pairs:

```rust
pub struct BuilderProvider<'a> {
    name: &'a str,
    api_key: Option<&'a ApiKey>,
}

pub async fn review_text(..., builder: &BuilderProvider<'_>) -> ...
```

Internal methods take &BuilderProvider; the .name() and .api_key() accessors give controlled access. ConfigError messages can attach the BuilderProvider for richer diagnostics.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
